reasoning:

The preceding 360-day average was 0.000 up to Oct 16, 2025. This means any single riot within the respective 30-day window will trigger a positive resolution. 

However, looking at the historical and current record up to October 26, 2025, riots at the UK bases in Cyprus (Akrotiri and Dhekelia) are exceedingly rare. For a riot to be recorded by ACLED, it must involve violent acts by three or more demonstrators (fights, rock throwing, etc.). While there have been fairly frequent anti-war and pro-Palestine protests outside RAF Akrotiri (dating from early 2024 through September/October 2025), they have consistently been peaceful demonstrations organized by peace groups (e.g., Cyprus Peace Council). Detailed searches returning reports of the late September 2025 and October 2025 protests show no evidence of violent clashes or arrests indicative of a riot. 

Given the base rate of 0 riots in the past 360 days, the lack of escalating violence in recent protests, and the policing standards at the bases, the probability of a riot suddenly occurring in these narrow 30-day windows is extremely low. For the November 2, 2025 resolution date, the relevant 30-day window (Oct 3 to Nov 2) has almost fully played out by our knowledge cutoff (Oct 26) with no known riots, so the probability is very close to zero (~4%). For the later dates (Nov 25, 2025, and Jan 24, 2026), there is slightly more unknown time for a protest dynamic to shift, but the baseline zero rate keeps the probabilities heavily anchored low (6% and 7% respectively).
